title: Improperly width'ed default function argument shouldn't error out

features.name: function default argument with undersized width
features.description: Code should include a function with a default argument value that is narrower than the parameter width (e.g., 1-bit literal for 32-bit parameter)
features.snippet:
function [31:0] foo (input [31:0] x = 1'b0); return x; endfunction
initial $display("%d", foo());

features.name: implicit width extension in function default argument
features.description: Code should include a function call that relies on implicit width extension of a default argument value
features.snippet:
function [31:0] foo (input [31:0] x = 1'b0); return x; endfunction
initial $display("%d", foo());
